summary.narrative: Greenberg campaigned on public safety, affordable housing, universal pre-K, gun control, and city infrastructure. Early term shows mixed results with documented wins in affordable housing (4,800+ units) and police reform (consent decree), but significant public dissatisfaction regarding public safety outcomes and overall promise fulfillment.
summary.grade_rationale: Moderate accomplishments in housing and police reform offset by persistent public safety concerns, budget management success, but missed/incomplete major platforms like universal pre-K and illegal gun crackdown specifics.

promises.normalized: Increase public safety and reduce violent crime
Create affordable housing units and homeownership access
Implement universal pre-K for all children
Crack down on illegal guns and gun violence

data_gaps: Specific universal pre-K enrollment numbers and implementation timeline
Detailed illegal gun seizure/prosecution metrics and enforcement strategy
Public safety outcome attribution (homicide decrease causation analysis)
Crime rate statistics for 2024-2025 comparison to campaign baseline
Economic development job creation verification and sector breakdown
Downtown revitalization Diamond District project completion status
Police consent decree enforcement progress and measurable reforms
Affordable housing breakdown by unit type and income qualification
